title: Host the full supported Pi RPC surface on AgentSessionRuntime
description: Replace the narrow prompt, steer, followUp, abort adapter with an in-process AgentSessionRuntime host compatible with stock Pi RPC semantics. Cover prompt and images, queue modes, state and messages, model and max thinking control, compaction and retry, session stats, commands, entries and tree cursors, naming, new, switch, fork, clone, import and export, bash where policy permits, extension UI request and response, agent_settled, entry_appended, and session replacement rebinding. Maintain typed capability discovery and conformance fixtures against the pinned Pi RPC protocol rather than importing Cacophony types.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: [broken-on-main] bootstrap CLI SIGTERM race returns signal exit
description:
`nix flake check` and a direct Nix package-check retry both fail `serve bootstraps an empty standalone instance before constructing the Pi factory` at `test/bootstrap-cli.test.mjs:95`: the child exit event reports `exitCode === null` after SIGTERM rather than graceful code 0. The exact isolated Node test and full local `npm test` pass. Root cause: `serve` publishes/listens on the owner socket before `waitForSignal()` installs SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers, so a client that handshakes immediately can signal during that startup window and receive the platform default termination. Add an early bounded signal latch that is installed before listeners become visible, drains through the existing whole-deadline shutdown once startup ownership exists, and is disposed on startup failure/injected shutdown. Include deterministic race coverage. Observed while validating bd-5c06cd; pre-existing on main.

---

Duplicate of bd-6dc8cb
creator: aurora:pi-daemon:aurora-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-aur-0

title: Bound protocol event and response serialization before allocation
description: Resource-bound finding from bd-62e2c5. ConnectionWriter.send in src/server.ts calls encodeLine and Buffer.from on the complete response or SDK event before checking maxOutboundBytesPerConnection. Model message events are not journal-record bounded, and mapped Pi event payloads can contain full messages, tool results, or provider error data. Therefore the advertised outbound bound controls only queued bytes after an unbounded JSON serialization and allocation. Define per-event and per-response payload limits, use bounded serialization or deliberate truncation and typed overflow behavior, prevent non-serializable SDK payloads from disrupting session delivery, and add adversarial tests proving a large event cannot grow process memory or affect other sessions.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash TUI pane: build the responsive interactive styled-grid browser component

Measure monospace cells and use ResizeObserver to derive/rate-limit bounded rows/columns; render changed styled row segments as virtualized DOM runs with cursor, selection, overlays and safe image placeholders plus accessible text mirror; send controller keyboard/paste/resize while observers remain read-only. One session's multiple panes mirror a canonical view, focused controller determines dimensions, and switching presentation retains focus/scroll/session state. Handle reconnection/snapshots/gaps and controller conflict beautifully. Meet 60fps resize/stream, bounded DOM/memory, keyboard/a11y and Nord Midnight visual regression.

Findings sent to bd-62e2c5 owner include explicit atomic snapshot-plus-stream attach, durable CRUD for dormant sessions, async message acceptance and request lookup, full AgentSessionRuntime/RPC control parity, neutral whole-service bearer auth, REST and NDJSON equivalence, APC/ACP adapter work, and a neutral stock-Pi or pico-style attach client. title: Preserve Pi conversation identity across restart and runtime replacement
description: Release-blocking persistence defect found by bd-62e2c5. Durable manifests currently restore session mode new by calling SessionManager.create again and restore memory mode as an empty in-memory session, so a logical session can lose its Pi conversation history across daemon restart while queued wakes replay into the wrong context. Move each slot to AgentSessionRuntime, persist the actual active Pi session file and replacement metadata, reject or clearly mark ephemeral modes, rebind events and extensions after new, switch, fork, clone, or import, and add real SDK restart and dormant-reopen tests proving conversation identity and entry continuity.

title: Bound recovery and shutdown and make readiness truthful
description: Startup currently waits for all queued model turns before listening, marks the host ready before per-session recovery finishes, and loses recovery failures after the ready log. Probe returns zero for any successful handshake without checking model, auth, recovery, or degraded state. Idle sweep failures can become unhandled rejections, adapter disposal can hang shutdown beyond the drain deadline, and readiness drains auth errors destructively while exposing private paths. Add bounded or background recovery admission, durable degraded and indeterminate counters, nondestructive redacted health, meaningful probe exit codes and timeouts, bounded dispose and extension shutdown, structured fatal handling, and tests for provider outage, corrupt session, hung adapter, and signal deadlines.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash workspace panes: restore single-pane scrolling/composer and repaint horizontal resize
description:
Confirmed production UX regression on current Dash: when one chat session occupies the full RHS workspace, transcript cannot be scrolled and footer/composer is not reachable or interactive. After splitting top/bottom, each pane becomes vertically scrollable. With a left/right (horizontal) split, content keeps its pre-split measured width instead of reflowing/repainting, so each side is chopped rather than wrapped/remeasured. Fix grid/flex min-height/min-width/overflow containment across workspace-pane/chat-pane/transcript/footer, ensure the single leaf fills the bounded workspace while transcript alone scrolls and composer stays visible/focusable, and trigger TanStack Virtual/CodeMirror/TUI/ResizeObserver measurement when pane dimensions or split ratios change. Horizontal splits must reflow rich rows/tool cards/code and TUI dimensions to each pane width without clipping; vertical splits must preserve independent scroll. Add production-shape Playwright regressions: one-pane long transcript scroll+composer input/send; horizontal split resize width reflow for both panes; vertical split independent scroll; repeated drag/keyboard resize under <16ms frame-work budget. Test responsive/mobile and no duplicate runtime/channel.

Operator also confirmed two adjacent production failures to fix in this same direct hotfix: (1) external sessions only showed preview-only/no co-opt buttons because the test instance's approved cwd root excluded recorded Pi CWDs; test config has now been explicitly widened to owner home and API reports direct/fork/preview eligibility—preserve clear reason text when genuinely ineligible. (2) Settings category buttons (`Editor & keys`, `Transcript`, `Cache & limits`) are inert; implement real tab state, keyboard/ARIA tab semantics, scoped content for every category using current revisioned settings, and Playwright coverage instead of decorative buttons.

Implemented directly by ms-mac worker; landed as main 3a41e06. Root cause for width clipping was intrinsic CSS Grid track sizing: fractional tracks retained child min-content widths, and the chat implicit column similarly exceeded its pane. Workspace/split/leaf/chat now have explicit bounded dimensions and zero-minimum tracks; rich virtualizer remeasures on nonzero pane resizes without measuring hidden TUI layers. Single full-width chat keeps transcript scrolling and composer pinned. Settings now has real ARIA tablist/tab/tabpanel state for Appearance, Editor & keys, Transcript, and Cache & limits, with revisioned existing settings controls. Production-shape Playwright regressions added. Validation: web Vitest 61/61, Playwright 16/16, npm audit 0, Nix flake check canonical npm 356/356. The ms-mac exact-main test instance is running 3a41e06 from /nix/store/glav12w52yhqni4glyfx33zpp5sv73rs-pi-daemon-0.1.0; API reports direct/fork/preview eligibility after the operator-authorized owner-home root update.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: CLI probe crashes on closed stdout pipe instead of handling EPIPE
description: While verifying bd-594877, piping `pi-daemon-test status` through `head` closed stdout while the helper's installed `pi-daemon probe` was emitting JSON. Node raised an unhandled `error` event on stdout (`Error: write EPIPE` from dist/cli.js io.stdout -> runProbe), printed a stack, and exited noisily. Reproduce with an active daemon: `pi-daemon probe --socket PATH | head -n 1` (or another early-closing consumer). CLI stdout/stderr should install bounded EPIPE handling and exit quietly/successfully or with a documented pipe status, without stack traces; add cross-command regression coverage. This is unrelated to service correctness and did not affect the running test instance.

title: [broken-on-main] SchedulerRuntime settle misses nested queue-one work
description: True main 32c3d4b intermittently fails `test/scheduler-runtime.test.mjs` test `overlap policies are bounded and queue-one coalesces to one deferred admission` in loaded `nix flake check`: expected the deferred second admission but observed only one. Prior bd-f86c45 added `SchedulerRuntime.settle()`, but it first awaits `#tail` and only then snapshots `#settlements`; a settlement can delete itself and enqueue a new serialized tail during that await, so settle observes an empty set and returns before the nested queue-one admission. Make settle loop over stable tail identity plus all settlement promises until no newer tail or settlement remains; add a deterministic regression that forces this interleaving. Observed while validating bd-96c3e1; failure is pre-existing/unrelated to lazy drafts.
creator: aurora:pi-daemon:aurora-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-aur-2

title: Document clean-install check after dependency-changing rebases
description: While validating bd-5f9ca1 after rebasing onto bd-2756e4, the checkout's stale `node_modules` produced TS2307 for a newly added exact root dependency even though `package.json` and `package-lock.json` were correct. This was initially misclassified as broken main and caused avoidable peer routing; `npm ci --ignore-scripts` resolved the import, after which only the owning lane's genuine ambient package-root test issue remained. Add an actionable contributor/agent check: when a rebase changes npm dependency metadata and compilation reports an unresolved new package, compare root package/lock and run the clean exact-lock install before filing broken-on-main. Preserve the separate rule that source or lock omissions still route to the owning bead. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-5f9ca1 session.

title: Dash lifecycle and packaging: embedded serve, `pi-daemon web`, Home Manager and self-contained assets
description:
Wire DashboardServer into `pi-daemon serve` embedded lifecycle and add standalone `pi-daemon web` over RemoteDashboardBackend. Resolve config/port defaults (API 7463, embedded 7464, dedicated 7465), auth sources, health/readiness, startup failure, drain/shutdown and signal behavior without extending the daemon whole deadline. Package one production content-hashed SPA under dist/web in npm pack and Nix; no runtime Vite/npm/CDN. Extend CLI help/docs and Home Manager for embedded/dedicated services, ordering, unique ports/state/token/log paths, secrets outside argv/Nix store, systemd/launchd/supervisord, multi-instance collision assertions, and Nix-on-Droid cache build. Add clean-install execution and service checks.

Parallelization note: embedded `serve` browser-listener lifecycle is split into operator-directed bd-b9d8e6 and may land while RemoteDashboardBackend is still in progress. This parent retains dedicated `pi-daemon web`, Home Manager, and final packaging/dual-mode closure.

title: Upgrade pinned Pi SDK after nested shrinkwrap security fixes
description: Fresh npm advisory data on 2026-07-20 reports GHSA-3jxr-9vmj-r5cp in @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent@0.80.6 nested brace-expansion 5.0.6 (fixed 5.0.7) and GHSA-j3f2-48v5-ccww in nested protobufjs 7.6.4 (fixed 7.6.5). Root npm overrides and package-lock edits do not replace these packages because the pinned Pi SDK publishes its own npm-shrinkwrap; attempting root lock surgery makes audit metadata claim green while npm ci still installs vulnerable versions, so that unsafe workaround was reverted. Update the upstream Pi SDK shrinkwrap/release, then bump Pi Daemon's exact Pi package pins and Nix hash, run contract/full host tests, and restore a truthful zero-audit baseline. No known external untrusted pattern/proto input path has been established yet; treat as dependency availability risk pending upstream.

title: Bootstrap private service state, bearer, and Pi auth on first launch
description: Direct operator request: a configured pi-daemon service must be able to start from an otherwise empty per-instance state rather than requiring Home Manager or shell commands to pre-create every path and credential copy. Implement bounded, fail-closed first-launch bootstrap for private state/socket/agent/log-relevant directories; atomically generate an owner-only API bearer when an explicitly configured token file is absent without overwriting/rotating existing credentials; and seed an absent per-instance Pi auth.json from an explicit owner-private regular seed file (with a safe standalone default where appropriate). Never put bearer/auth bytes in argv, Nix store, logs, status, manifests, or responses. Reject symlinks, permissive/foreign-owned files and dirs, ambiguous sources, overlap/path traversal, and partial/racy initialization. Preserve existing explicit file/fd/environment bearer behavior and no-tools defaults. Wire the Home Manager module to the bootstrap contract, add first-launch/restart/concurrency/adversarial tests, and update quickstart/operations/security/PLAN.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Make clean npm packages build and execute the installed Pi Daemon CLI
description: Release-blocking distribution defects reproduced by the SDK audit. The npm bin symlink silently exits because src/cli.ts compares non-canonical process.argv path with import.meta.url, and npm pack from a clean checkout can omit dist entirely because there is no prepack build. Canonicalize entrypoint detection or use a dedicated bin wrapper, add a prepack or prepare build that is safe under npm ci ignore-scripts policy, export the protocol schema subpath, and add a clean tarball content test plus installed npm exec and direct bin smoke that starts a daemon and performs version or probe. Verify the release workflow uses the same artifact.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash BFF stream router: bridge authenticated browser frames to DashboardBackend channels
description: Parallel slice of bd-ea2019 after operator requested Aurora help. Implement a transport-neutral DashboardStreamHandler/router for `/dash/v1/stream` that maps the existing versioned browser client frames to one injected DashboardBackend: bounded open/close rich and TUI channels, command/control/extension-UI/input/resize correlation, snapshot/delta/control/replay-gap/server-error frames, exact identity+generation, per-peer/channel limits, cleanup on socket close/logout/server drain, no accepted-command replay, and no service bearer in browser code/state. Use DashboardWebSocketPeer only through its public bounded send/message/close seam and authenticate through the existing DashboardServer cookie boundary. Provide factory/injection for embedded lifecycle; dedicated lifecycle can inject RemoteDashboardBackend later. Add deterministic fake-backend and real in-process conformance tests including reconnect gaps, controller conflict, malformed/oversized frames, slow/error isolation and complete teardown. Do not edit React SPA/TuiGrid/visual files (msm2 owns browser client) or RemoteDashboardBackend (msm1 owns).

title: Dash core: project Pi JSONL trees into fast paged semantic transcripts
description: Implement TranscriptProjector and projection cache from the versioned Dash contract. Correctly parse Pi session v1/v2/v3, migrate in memory, follow the active leaf, preserve branch/compaction/summary/label/model/thinking/session-info/custom semantics, normalize messages/images/tool calls/results, page newest viewport first, and reconcile entry IDs with live events. Bound file/line/entry/image/output/time/memory, process large files off the daemon event-loop hot path, fingerprint/invalidate projections, and atomically cache only safe normalized data. Provide rich fixtures including malformed/orphaned trees and performance tests meeting cached <150ms/cold normal <500ms useful viewport targets. Parsing must never hydrate SDK/provider/extensions or send a turn.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Allow explicit high-trust authority-root overlap for home cwd sessions
description: Pi Daemon correctly rejects home-directory sessions because cwd contains daemon state/session/Pi credential roots, but the operator explicitly runs Pi from $HOME and accepts this same-user trust tradeoff. Add a default-false security.allowAuthorityRootOverlap YAML option plus matching serve CLI and Home Manager instance option. When true, PiSessionFactory may open/fork/direct sessions whose cwd/session root overlaps daemon state or selected Pi credential roots, while retaining canonical allowedRoots, owner/mode/symlink, source fingerprint, controller, and conflict checks. Document prominently that any enabled bash/host/session tool can then access protected paths beneath cwd. Keep default behavior unchanged and add config/factory/module tests. Enable it only in the operator isolated test config after exact-main deployment, then verify the reported TwinFlames external session can Safe fork and Direct co-opt without modifying the source unexpectedly.
creator: helsinki:pi-daemon:harry

title: Add a first-class Pi SDK per-session environment and provider-registry seam
description: While implementing bd-ab1b91, Pi Daemon could scope known provider API-key auth and bash child env through public APIs, but arbitrary extensions, pi.exec, custom provider interpolation, OAuth/ADC helpers, and provider registration still read or mutate process-wide state. Investigate or upstream an explicit environment/provider-registry injection object accepted by createAgentSessionServices so concurrent in-process runtimes can consume caller-supplied values without process.env swapping or global registry resets. Keep unisolated semantics until the seam is real. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-ab1b91 session.

title: Dash follow-on: declarative cross-mode rich extension renderer contract
description: After Dash v1, design/propose a safe versioned declarative extension view model shared where sensible across TUI/RPC/web: bounded text/markdown/code/diff/image/key-value/status/stack/grid/action/form primitives, capability scoping, renderer provenance, input correlation and safe fallback. Prefer upstream Pi proposal. Validate server-side trees; never download/eval extension JavaScript in browser. Integrate rich renderer without replacing shadow-TUI compatibility.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash direct co-opt: support normal owner-owned Pi session directories safely
description: Direct co-opt of an old normal Pi session fails with insecure_state_path / “Pi session directory must be owner-only”. SessionOwnershipService intentionally uses dirname(source.canonicalPath) for direct mode, then PiAdapter calls ensurePrivateDirectory on that source directory and rejects ordinary Pi-created 0755 roots such as ~/.pi/agent/sessions/project, even when the directory and 0600 JSONL are owned by the current user and are not group/world-writable. Fork works only because it uses a daemon-owned 0700 directory. Preserve direct co-opt semantics without silently weakening daemon-state security: distinguish external source authority from daemon state, require current-user ownership plus no group/world write and regular non-symlink source files, or offer an explicit consented permission-migration path. Never chmod ambient roots silently. Return a precise typed conflict with actionable fork/permission guidance. Add acceptance using a representative normal Pi 0755 directory + private JSONL, writer conflict checks, symlink/foreign/group-writable rejection, and exact direct append/export behavior. Operator reproduction on rolling test instance 2026-07-21; source anchors src/session-ownership.ts direct sessionDir selection and src/pi-adapter.ts ensurePrivateDirectory.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1

Produce working Nord Midnight screens for sidebar/chat/tool calls/info/settings/splits, keyboard and accessibility behavior, measured bundle size, first paint, search, streaming render, resize/swap animation, memory, and 60fps traces. title: Add neutral persisted per-session schedules and durable wakes
description:
Implement a standalone Pi Daemon scheduler for per-session recurring wakes, independent of Cacophony. Persist versioned schedules with enabled state, validated cron expression, IANA timezone, bounded prompt, optional model/thinking override, overlap policy, missed-wake/catch-up policy, jitter, next/last run, and safe terminal ticket summary. Submit every trigger through the existing durable prompt admission/ticket path; preserve queued/accepted/completed/failed/indeterminate semantics and never blind-replay accepted work after a crash. Add bounded scheduler lifecycle, clock/timezone/DST/restart/overlap handling, HTTP/CLI/config CRUD, capability/status fields, authorization and redacted audit logging. Expose authoritative nextWakeAt and schedule history to Pi Daemon Dash for dark-magenta countdown/liveness state plus create/edit/enable/disable UX. External timer clients remain supported; native scheduling is additive. Detailed design: web/PLAN.md sections 12.1 and 24 Phase 5.

title: Restore Dash session across browser reload without storing credential
description: A valid HttpOnly Max-Age Dash cookie survives reload, but BrowserDashboardClient loses its memory-only CSRF token and ProductionApp incorrectly routes an authenticated successful bootstrap back to login because authenticatedForMutations remains false. Do not put the owner credential or cookie in localStorage/sessionStorage/JavaScript. Add a same-origin authenticated bootstrap CSRF refresh: rotate the server-side CSRF digest for the existing bounded browser session, return the new token in a no-store response header or typed bootstrap-only field, have the browser client adopt it after successful cookie-authenticated bootstrap, and enter ready state. Preserve exact Host/Origin/cookie rules, HttpOnly/SameSite, bounded session capacity/TTL, restart revocation, logout, and cross-origin unreadability. Add server/auth/client/unit/Playwright regression proving ordinary reload requires no credential while daemon restart/expired/revoked cookie still returns login.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Implement explicit multi-reader attach with snapshot, replay, and gaps
description: Provide session rpc attachment over the additive API and a corresponding explicit attach and detach operation for NDJSON. Attach must atomically return the current session and request snapshot plus a live event boundary; support multiple bounded readers, origin-routed command responses, event sequence cursors, bounded replay after sequence, an explicit gap requiring resync, host restart identity, ping or keepalive, typed detach versus session exit, and reconnect. Define one-controller or first-response semantics for extension UI dialogs while allowing many observers. Add two-client attach, disconnect, reconnect, slow-reader, host-restart, and session-replacement acceptance tests.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash server: implement secure browser auth, static SPA host, workspace and settings stores
description: Implement DashboardServer's browser-facing shell independent of session backend completion. Serve content-hashed local assets with strict CSP and security headers; add web-token login exchange to revocable signed HttpOnly SameSite cookies; exact Host/Origin/CSRF checks; bounded HTTP/WS admission; no daemon bearer in browser memory/storage. Implement owner-private revisioned workspace split-tree and UI-only runtime settings stores under STATE_DIR/web, effective defaults/source reporting, reset, atomic recovery/corruption/bounds, and safe bootstrap. title: [broken-on-main] bootstrap-cli serve exit status failing
description:
On main d6cd751, node --test test/bootstrap-cli.test.mjs reproducibly fails at line 95: child exitCode is null rather than 0 after SIGTERM. Full npm test otherwise passes. Agent bd-6d96bb is taking the fix per broken-on-main workflow.

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-03b0d2

Title: [broken-on-main] bootstrap CLI SIGTERM race returns signal exit

`nix flake check` and a direct Nix package-check retry both fail `serve bootstraps an empty standalone instance before constructing the Pi factory` at `test/bootstrap-cli.test.mjs:95`: the child exit event reports `exitCode === null` after SIGTERM rather than graceful code 0. The exact isolated Node test and full local `npm test` pass. Root cause: `serve` publishes/listens on the owner socket before `waitForSignal()` installs SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers, so a client that handshakes immediately can signal during that startup window and receive the platform default termination. Add an early bounded signal latch that is installed before listeners become visible, drains through the existing whole-deadline shutdown once startup ownership exists, and is disposed on startup failure/injected shutdown. Include deterministic race coverage. Observed while validating bd-5c06cd; pre-existing on main.

description:

Focused 17/17 and HM module check green; a real 180MiB production-dependency install from a locally packed+checksummed shrinkwrap artifact successfully launched version 0.1.0. Implementation complete at b9fb60b. Validation: ambient npm 366/366, focused self-update/release 18/18, npm audit zero, real 180 MiB production dependency install from a locally checksummed shrinkwrapped release artifact launched pi-daemon 0.1.0, and exact Nix outputs are valid for package /nix/store/qmhdyxhx0ypg0npga9fwnz6gnr4z9jsh-pi-daemon-0.1.0, Home Manager module /nix/store/lwr1fbv1kbc0y82i2ki72332ixd76sq7-pi-daemon-home-manager-module-check, and Pages /nix/store/pfim1pp8p5zk4xpvnahpg9qm9k7cmwa2-pi-daemon-pages. title: Dash remote backend: implement REST/framed-RPC parity, reconnect and channel coalescing
description: Implement RemoteDashboardBackend for `pi-daemon web`. Use the authenticated neutral Session API plus `pi-daemon-rpc.v1`; keep daemon bearer server-side. Satisfy the same backend conformance suite as embedded mode for list/info/transcript/ownership/export, hydration, session state/entries/stats/commands/models, controller roles, prompts/steer/follow-up/abort, extension UI, presence and warm leases. Coalesce pane subscriptions, bound in-flight/outbound/reconnect state, retain cursors, resync gaps, reject blind command replay, and surface compatibility/capability failures. Dedicated extra hop must preserve responsiveness and measured <50ms local stream overhead.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Split RemoteDashboardBackend Rich and TUI hubs into focused transport modules
description: While implementing bd-ad4630, `src/dashboard-remote-backend.ts` grew to contain neutral REST delegation, transcript page assembly, framed-RPC hub/channel state, TUI hub/channel state, reconnect policy, cursor replay, and shared validation helpers in one large module. The behavior is bounded and tested, but future protocol changes will be easier to review if the Rich and TUI transport hubs move into internal focused modules while `RemoteDashboardBackend` remains the public orchestration/export seam. Preserve the shared parameterized conformance tests and avoid changing public package behavior. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-ad4630 session.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1

title: Dash follow-on: multi-user identities and per-session authorization
description: Post-v1 epic. Replace the single-operator web trust domain with explicit identities, browser sessions, roles, per-session/read/control/admin authorization, auditable controller transfer, workspace ownership/sharing and revocation while retaining service-to-service daemon bearer isolation. Requires a separate threat model, credential provider contract, migration and exhaustive no-existence-leak/security tests; do not retrofit ad hoc ACL fields into v1.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash embedded lifecycle slice: start the packaged browser server from pi-daemon serve
description: Operator-directed parallel slice of bd-31ee8f so the already-built visual Dash is actually openable while the remote backend lands. Wire DashboardServer into `pi-daemon serve` when validated `web.enabled` is true, constructing the in-process DashboardBackend from the same inventory/projector/ownership/multiplexer services without bypassing policy. Start only after the owner Unix/API services are ready; include address/readiness in safe logs/status, fail startup atomically, and stop it inside the existing whole shutdown deadline. Preserve loopback-only browser auth/BFF, generated owner-private web token, packaged content-hashed SPA, and current capability-gated TUI unavailability until ShadowTuiHost is injected. Add subprocess/lifecycle tests proving `127.0.0.1:7474` serves the SPA/login while bearer never reaches browser output, collisions fail closed, SIGINT/SIGTERM drain all listeners, primary/test instances remain isolated, and no runtime Vite/npm/CDN exists. Do not add dedicated `pi-daemon web`, RemoteDashboardBackend, Home Manager dedicated services, or live SPA data plumbing; those remain bd-ad4630/bd-31ee8f/bd-ea2019.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Build Pi Daemon Dash: beautiful fast browser session workspace
description:
Deliver the complete Pi Daemon Dash architecture in web/PLAN.md: one self-contained, exceptionally fast and visually polished SPA; searchable recent-first Pi session inventory; preview-first transcript rendering; explicit direct/fork/export session ownership; embedded and dedicated backends over one behavioral contract; live Pi streaming and controls; persistent mouse/keyboard split-pane workspace; semantic Nord Midnight theming; rich browser and shadow-TUI per-pane modes; secure browser BFF; bounded caches and warm residency; exact npm/Nix/Home Manager packaging. Performance and visual quality are product requirements: local sidebar/bootstrap should feel instantaneous, transcript paint should precede SDK hydration, streaming should update within a frame, layout motion should remain 60fps, and every intermediate state must be intentional and beautiful. Existing Pi Daemon security/durability/RPC/ACP/package gates must remain green. This epic closes only when the final browser/security/performance/Nix acceptance bead lands.

Dependency graph materialized on 2026-07-18. Final closure gate: bd-7de9ec. Detailed bead mapping lives in web/PLAN.md section 24.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Fix Linux package-bin acceptance and make Pages Docker-free with Nix
description:
Linux x86_64 Nix/CI fails package.test because the staged installed .bin/pi-daemon executable is ENOENT. GitHub Pages also invokes actions/jekyll-build-pages, which requires a missing Docker daemon on the self-hosted runner. Reproduce/fix the cross-platform package staging/bin link and replace container-backed Pages build with a pinned Nix-native static site build/upload/deploy path. Preserve self-hosted-only policy and run Linux-representative package plus workflow checks.

Follow-up after the Linux/Pages fixes: the latest main CI run 29368198425 has its macOS Nix job stuck in nix flake check for more than 12 hours because the workflow has no job/step deadline. Add explicit bounded CI timeouts, cancel/rerun the stale run, and verify a terminal all-green main workflow.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-2

title: Dash rich transcript: render Pi conversations and tools beautifully at streaming speed
description: Implement the production virtualized rich transcript over normalized projector/live fixtures. Render user images/text, assistant markdown/thinking/errors/usage/streaming cursor, queued messages, tool start/update/end, built-in read/bash/edit-diff/write/grep/find/ls presentations, generic custom tools, bash execution, compaction/branch summaries, labels/model/thinking/name markers, custom messages/entries, retries and errors. Merge persisted/live/entry_appended records idempotently by IDs and reconcile replay gaps without duplicates. Lazy-highlight/sanitize/collapse huge content and images; use semantic theme tokens only. Meet <16ms frame work and bounded DOM/memory under long rapid streams, with polished partial/success/error/empty states and visual regression fixtures.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Scheduler browser BFF: add deployment-neutral schedule resources to DashboardBackend and /dash/v1
description: Unblock bd-edbc79 without exposing the daemon service bearer to JavaScript. Extend the deployment-neutral DashboardBackend with capability-gated schedule list/get/create/update/delete/status methods using existing schedule contract resources, ETag/revision/idempotency and content-safe responses. Implement embedded delegation to the local schedule controller/store and RemoteDashboardBackend delegation through new bounded SessionApiClient schedule methods. Add authenticated same-origin `/dash/v1/schedules` BFF routes with exact Host/Origin/CSRF, body/count/response bounds, prompt input-only handling, and older-daemon typed capability absence. Browser client may call only these cookie BFF routes. Add shared embedded/remote conformance, server auth/security, reconnect/version and package/schema/docs tests. Do not edit React visual components (c0cjbrkskgmk7776 owns scheduler UX), timer runtime, or service-bearer route semantics.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Scheduler runtime: durable timer loop and prompt-ticket admission
description: Implement the bounded scheduler engine over bd-6d96bb. Maintain next-fire heap/timers, recompute on CRUD/clock changes, submit through existing durable prompt ticket admission, enforce same-session overlap policy, jitter/max delay and missed-wake/catch-up bounds. Persist trigger state before admission; retain queued/running/succeeded/failed/indeterminate truth; never blind-replay accepted work after crash. Handle disabled/deleted/dormant/failed sessions, daemon drain/restart, monotonic versus wall clock, cancellation and resource limits. Add deterministic fake-clock, crash-window, overlap, backlog, memory and long-soak tests.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Add a strong isolation backend for mutually untrusted Pi sessions
description: The completed v0.1 host truthfully supports only in-process isolation.mode=unisolated: AgentSession state/queues are separate, but process.env, cwd, module globals, arbitrary extensions, custom providers, filesystem/process/network authority, and crash containment share one Node trust domain. Design and implement an explicitly advertised process, container, microVM, or equivalent backend for mutually untrusted sessions, including per-session env/credential projection, canonical cwd/filesystem policy, bounded IPC/lifecycle/recovery, capability negotiation, packaging, and adversarial cross-session leakage tests. Do not rename unisolated or imply a sandbox until the boundary is real. Coordinate with bd-46eb52's upstream in-process environment/provider-registry seam rather than treating that seam as strong isolation.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash SPA shell: ship the measured fast app, sidebar, info view and semantic theme foundation
description: Turn the accepted frontend spike and browser contract fixtures into the production SPA shell. Build content-hashed/lazy assets, application layout, accessible/responsive virtualized searchable expandable session sidebar, recent-first rows with name/first-prompt title, age, cwd, info hover/focus popover and info-pane selection, liveness/attention visuals, loading/empty/error/skeleton states, and semantic Nord Midnight CSS token/theme foundation. Preserve first-paint and 60fps budgets with 10k fixture sessions, keyboard and screen-reader behavior, no CDN, and no hardcoded component colors. This bead may use mock backend fixtures but must expose typed data adapters for integration.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: [broken-on-main] background recovery test timing out under Nix
description: nix flake check under Node 24 reaches durability background replay after the fixed ~200ms poll window; adapter.calls remains 0 at test/durability.test.mjs:477 although local npm passes. Replace attempt-count poll with a bounded 2s deadline.
creator: aurora:pi-daemon:zt8y8mat8uodv9g3

title: Track Pi default session-directory encoding through an exported compatibility helper
description: Context: bd-fd9f22 needs to place new/imported Dash sessions in stock Pi's cwd-encoded `<agentDir>/sessions/--...--` directory. Pi 0.80.6 implements and exports `getDefaultSessionDir()` from its internal `core/session-manager` module, but the package root does not re-export it and package exports forbid the subpath. Pi Daemon therefore had to reproduce the short path-encoding algorithm locally, creating upgrade drift risk. Add a pinned compatibility assertion and, when the supported Pi SDK exposes the helper publicly, consume that export; alternatively propose the root re-export upstream. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-fd9f22 session.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1

title: Ship a Pi-RPC stdio bridge and remote attach client
description: Provide the lowest-risk operator and compatibility path first: ship a pi-daemon-rpc-style binary that reads and writes stock Pi RPC JSONL on stdio and bridges to an authenticated session rpc attachment by ID or name. Existing pico and pi-acp-style clients can then treat it like pi --mode rpc while the daemon remains processless per session. Add reconnect and gap status, terminal lifecycle, correlation routing, installed-package smoke, and Cacophony pico compatibility fixtures without importing Cacophony. After bridge parity, evaluate a Pi package extension registering connect that intercepts input and renders the remote transcript; stock Pi currently has no simple remote AgentSession replacement seam, so a bespoke attach TUI or upstream seam may be safer than pretending local UI parity.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash TUI core: host one injected VirtualTerminal view per session and stream bounded grids
description:
Implement daemon ShadowTuiHost from the accepted spike/upstream seam. Bind one canonical interactive virtual view to the resident AgentSessionRuntime/extension instance; never spawn Pi or create a second writer. Support session replacement/rebind, controller dimensions/input, observer mirrors, mutable bounded rows/columns, ANSI-to-styled-grid deltas, cursor/title, overlays/widgets/custom tool/message/entry renderers/editor, safe image placeholders, side-channel stripping, replay/snapshot/gaps, leases and disposal. Expose neutral TUI channel through embedded backend and authenticated API so dedicated mode has parity. Prove rapid resize/input/custom extensions do not duplicate runtimes or block model events and meet <50ms delta target.

Spike handoff (bd-2756e4): use exported `VirtualTerminal` and `docs/shadow-tui.md`. Pi 0.80.6 component rendering is proven, with npm/Nix p95 receipts and OSC52/image/device stripping. Full extension mode must wait for or carry the documented host-safe `InteractiveSessionView` seam: injectable Terminal/lifecycle/tool resolution, external extension binding, exposed stable ExtensionUIContext/render trigger. Do not instantiate current `InteractiveMode`: it hardcodes ProcessTerminal/process handlers/ensureTool and its bind path re-emits session_start. One controlling view owns the stable extension UI broker; panes beyond it are frame observers.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Make Nix-on-Droid package builds avoid native npm double-free
description: Operator reproduced Pi Daemon package failure during Astra/SGU24 nix-on-droid switch. Native aarch64 Android build reaches buildNpmPackage npmConfigHook `npm ci --ignore-scripts` and aborts with `double free or corruption (out)`, causing pi-daemon, home-manager-path, supervisord config, and the generation to fail. Collective previously documented the npm double-free as resolved only by building off-device and pulling from cache, but the cache miss for pi-daemon d18854f exposed the native fallback. Make the Pi Daemon Nix consumer/package contract Nix-on-Droid-safe: avoid native npm execution on Android (prefer deterministic off-device/cross-built or reusable fixed package artifact), preserve exact package/lock integrity, and provide an actionable cache/prebuild path. Validate aarch64-linux package output and Nix-on-Droid module evaluation for Astra/SGU24 without weakening Linux/macOS checks. Document the platform contract and failure recovery. Evidence derivation jlrjh1zz80x4psibas42wa9l2ps49djz-pi-daemon-0.1.0.drv; npm ci aborts in npm-config-hook after cache setup.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Implement a durable resident and dormant session catalog with CRUD
description: Current host status and close operate only on resident SessionSlot objects; idle eviction makes retained sessions invisible and undeletable. Build a bounded durable catalog keyed by immutable ID with optional unique name, generation, cwd, Pi session target, runtime configuration digest, isolation mode, resident or dormant state, timestamps, and last terminal state. Implement create, list, get, mutable update where safe, reopen, close, and delete for both resident and dormant sessions with pagination and crash-safe metadata. Emit explicit dormant, evicted, reopened, renamed, and deleted lifecycle events. Keep catalog data neutral and exclude Cacophony objects.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash activated sessions: add explicit trusted extension and resource policy
description: Dash direct/fork activation currently hardcodes tools:none and empty extensions/skills/promptTemplates/themes in EmbeddedDashboardServiceRuntime.runtimeSpec. Consequently reviewed operator extensions such as the agent-utils /m model shortcut never load, and the UI gives no indication that activation intentionally stripped them. Do not inherit arbitrary ambient project extensions into the shared daemon trust domain. Instead add an explicit bounded trusted resource profile selected from daemon configuration and surfaced in activation/new-session UI: reviewed extension/package allowlist, skills/templates/themes policy, tool-adapter policy, and a visible effective-resource summary. Embedded and dedicated backends must apply the same policy; unknown paths/packages fail closed; browser input cannot inject extension paths or environment secrets. Add acceptance that the configured agent-utils package loads its approved commands (including /m), unapproved extensions remain absent, reload/reconnect does not duplicate registrations, and default no-tools/no-extensions remains secure when no profile is configured. Operator reproduction 2026-07-21.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1

title: Dash embedded backend: bind direct services without bypassing daemon policy
description: Implement InProcessDashboardBackend over SessionInventory, TranscriptProjector, SessionOwnershipService, Multiplexer, tickets, RPC controller/attachments, liveness and warm leases. It must satisfy the DashboardBackend conformance fixtures exactly, use normal generation/controller/idempotency/turn scheduling and serialization boundaries, hydrate without prompting, coalesce one session channel across panes, maintain bounded presence/seen cursors, and release leases/channels on disconnect. Direct calls are an optimization only; no transport-only validation, auth-independent path authority, or duplicate session state machine may be bypassed.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash config foundation: add instance YAML convention and safe precedence
description: Add a bounded general Pi Daemon YAML configuration contract at ~/.config/pi/daemon/<instance>/config.yaml. Selection precedence: --config, PI_DAEMON_CONFIG, validated --instance/PI_DAEMON_INSTANCE/default; individual CLI options override YAML. Map current serve socket/state/agent/allowed-root/limit/API/auth-source options without breaking CLI compatibility. Secrets remain file/fd/env references, never literal config values or Nix store content. Add web namespace types/defaults but not the web server. Extend Home Manager to select/generate non-secret instance config safely. Validate unknown fields, sizes/depth, symlinks/ownership where required, path expansion, duplicate CLI conflicts, and redacted errors. Document config versus STATE_DIR runtime overlay.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Expose an in-process ACP adapter at the session apc endpoint
description: Implement the operator-requested session apc endpoint while documenting that the upstream protocol and adapter are Agent Client Protocol, ACP, and pi-acp. Reuse or port the MIT pi-acp translation layer against each in-process AgentSessionRuntime instead of spawning pi --mode rpc. Cover ACP JSON-RPC routing, initialize and capabilities, session list or load or prompt, tools, permissions and client requests, commands and streaming updates, cancellation, multi-client behavior, bearer auth, durable session identity, and lifecycle parity with NDJSON and rpc attachment. Do not add Cacophony dependencies.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Specify additive session CRUD, RPC attach, and ACP API contracts
description: Define the versioned contract before implementation. Preserve existing Unix NDJSON commands as one control mode and add authenticated JSON endpoints for session collection and session by ID or name CRUD, rpc attachment, and the operator-requested apc path implementing upstream ACP. Specify request and event envelopes, HTTP or local transport mapping, error/status codes, session aliases and generations, pagination, capabilities, request tickets, attach cursors, snapshot/live atomicity, replay gaps, reconnect and host identity, multi-reader response routing, extension UI controller semantics, and equivalence rules between control modes. Publish JSON Schema plus OpenAPI or equivalent fixtures and compatibility tests. Child of the full standalone host epic.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Restrict GitHub CI to self-hosted runners
description:
Configure GitHub Actions CI workflows to run exclusively on self-hosted runners, matching the pattern used by the cacophony project and other caco projects.

Acceptance criteria:
- All CI workflow jobs use `runs-on` targeting the self-hosted runner label(s) rather than GitHub-hosted runners (e.g. ubuntu-latest).
- Configuration is consistent with how cacophony and other caco projects define their self-hosted runners.
- CI runs successfully on the self-hosted infrastructure after the change.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: [broken-on-main] test/package.test.mjs syntax error

title: [broken-on-main] 10k inventory bootstrap p95 exceeds 50ms under host load
description:
After bd-93e857 landed at 75d8b59, the exact test `10k persisted bootstrap and indexed request paths meet the contract budgets` passed locally and in Nix but failed in a peer full gate after rebase: bootstrap p95 53.47ms versus the 50ms contract (196/197 tests passed). The implementation owner is taking this repair; preserve the <50ms budget and fix remaining bootstrap I/O or measurement isolation without raising/skipping the threshold. Peer web-only bd-c0df45 tests remain green. A first create attempt timed out during daemon endpoint backpressure; exact title/text searches after recovery found no existing bead.

Peer post-fix full-suite validation after c458c6c still measured bootstrap p95 51.82ms (213/214), so this bug is reopened under the original owner. Second repair removes default-query normalization/digest/filter traversal and manually clones only the bounded hot rows; focused 10k result is 7.97ms while preserving the exact <50ms assertion. Will close again only after two loaded full-suite runs plus Nix are green.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1

title: Dash follow-on: native TLS and hardened remote browser deployment
description: Post-v1 optional native HTTPS/WSS surface. Define cert/key file/fd loading and rotation, SNI/Host/origin/public-base-url/proxy-header trust, secure cookies/HSTS, loopback/reverse-proxy compatibility, remote health and Home Manager secret paths without values in argv/Nix store/logs. Keep reverse proxy as initial recommendation. Add downgrade, mixed-content, spoofed-forwarded-header, cert rotation and multi-instance tests.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Make Dash workspace and settings idempotency keys reload-unique
description: Live production Dash settings reproduce 409 idempotency_conflict: useDashboardSettings and useDashboardWorkspace restart sequenceRef at zero on every page load, then reuse durable keys like settings-patch-1/workspace-save-1 with different bodies retained by the server. Appearance may appear to work only until a prior key collides; Transcript toggles and Cache inputs then remain unchanged. Give each hook instance a cryptographically random mutation scope and include it in request/idempotency keys while retaining ordered tails and revision handling. Add regression that two independently mounted/reloaded clients can issue different first settings/workspace mutations without key reuse, then verify live Transcript and Cache controls.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash new session: add lazy create flow with no runtime work before first message
description:
Production Dash has no way to create a new logical session from the browser. Add a prominent New Session action and bounded form for cwd/name plus allowlisted session policy/model/resource choices. Creation must be lazy: it may persist a validated dormant/catalog session or browser draft, but must not instantiate an AgentSessionRuntime, load SDK/provider/model/extensions/tools, acquire a controller, spawn a process, or submit work before the user sends the first message. The new pane should look like an ordinary empty conversation with the composer pinned to the pane bottom and an inline `first message starts this session` indicator. First send atomically materializes/hydrates the session through normal policy, obtains controller state, and admits exactly one prompt; retries must obey existing idempotent/indeterminate semantics and must never duplicate the first turn. Define the neutral backend/BFF/API contract needed for embedded and dedicated modes, enforce cwd/root/resource bounds and service/browser auth separation, and add acceptance proving zero runtime/SDK/model work before send, exactly one runtime+turn after send, refresh/restart durability, cancellation of an unsent draft, and no subprocess creation. Operator report 2026-07-19; current Sidebar has no create action and ordinary POST session currently opens a runtime immediately, so this requires an explicit lazy lifecycle rather than wiring the existing eager create blindly.

Decomposed 2026-07-19 after architecture audit: bd-6a4170 owns no-runtime contract/store/BFF CRUD; bd-96c3e1 owns exact-once embedded/remote first-send materialization; bd-72d6fd owns the SPA new-session UX and reuses bd-930d31 preview-composer semantics. This parent remains the final cross-mode acceptance/closure gate.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1

title: Dash live UI: connect preview, hydration, RPC streams, liveness and extension interactions
description:
Integrate the production SPA with DashboardServer and both backends. Preview paints first; activation direct/fork choice and export UX run via tickets; hydration sends no prompt; entries/state/stats/commands/models populate rich chips; controller/observer state gates input. Implement prompt/steer/follow-up/abort/model/thinking/name/compaction/command flows, slash completion, live assistant/tool/queue/retry events, reconnect/cursor/gap reconciliation, extension select/confirm/input/editor plus notifications/widgets/status, warm presence leases, exact grey/green/blue/magenta dots and white unread ring/seen cursors. Every waiting/conflict/preview-only/error/indeterminate state must be polished, fast and actionable.

Dependency audit 2026-07-18 (operator-directed): removed bd-ad4630 as a hard prerequisite. The SPA integrates only the deployment-neutral DashboardBackend contract and can implement/test the complete embedded live path against InProcessDashboardBackend while RemoteDashboardBackend lands in parallel; dedicated parity remains a final acceptance/packaging gate. No acceptance criterion is waived.

2026-07-18 parallelization: browser SPA/login/client state remains this bead; authenticated BFF stream frame↔DashboardBackend routing is split to Aurora bead bd-3a61f7. Both are required for live browser acceptance.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Dash imported sessions fail restart recovery despite existing managed JSONL
description: Rolling test instance on v0.2.2/current main remains degraded after restart with two retained imported Dash sessions. Recovery reports open_failed twice: dash-rZdce... -> ENOENT and dash-Y8_e... -> session_path_outside_state. Both ownership-v1 records are active/imported and both managedPath JSONL files still exist; provider registry is fully ready. This indicates retained catalog/runtime reconstruction is replaying the original fork/open target or losing resolved managed conversation identity instead of reopening the exact existing managed JSONL. Fix recovery so a succeeded fork/direct ownership mapping reopens its recorded managed conversation path/generation without source replay, prompt/model work, or blind mutation. Preserve durable indeterminate semantics and distinguish genuinely missing managed files from source resolution failures. Add restart acceptance for daemon-owned imported sessions and legacy retained records, including exact model/resource policy, no prompt, and ready host state. Do not delete or rewrite the two operator test records while diagnosing.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1

title: Scheduler Dash UX: beautiful per-session schedule editor, countdowns and history
description:
Add capability-gated schedule UI to Dash after scheduler API: create/edit cron with human preview, timezone/DST validation, prompt editor, model/thinking override, overlap/missed-wake/jitter controls, enable/disable/delete, next/last wake, durable ticket/history outcome and actionable errors. Drive dark-magenta dormant dot/countdown regardless focus and preserve white unread completion ring. Keep UI absent—not fake—against older daemons. Integrate settings/info pane, keyboard/a11y/responsive behavior, Nord Midnight semantic tokens, fast updates and visual regressions for validation/error/disabled/running/unseen states.

2026-07-19 security audit: browser cannot call service-bearer `/v1/schedule` directly. Deployment-neutral backend/BFF schedule resources are split to bd-c39242; this UX remains frontend-only and consumes only authenticated same-origin `/dash/v1` routes.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Surface redacted runCli fatal error codes in serve-test exit assertions
description: While completing bd-fc8275, the `serve CLI enables an ephemeral loopback API without logging the bearer` test failed only as `1 !== 0`; the captured `errors` array held the actionable `outbound_record_too_large` code but was not included in assertion diagnostics. Add a shared test helper or assertion message that reports only redacted event/error codes (never bearer values, temporary roots, prompts, or raw log bodies) when a `runCli(... serve ...)` exit code is unexpected. This should shorten diagnosis without weakening existing explicit token/path non-disclosure assertions. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-fc8275 session.
creator: aurora:pi-daemon:aurora-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-aur-2

title: [broken-on-main] SchedulerRuntime fake-clock tests race under loaded Nix suite
description: True main 50b7910 fails exact rolling `nix flake check` although isolated/Aurora gates passed. Nix full suite: test/scheduler-runtime.test.mjs `fake clock fires once, persists before admission, and retains terminal ticket truth` throws TypeError at line 102 reading undefined `.state`; `long fake-clock soak keeps timers, overlap state and retained memory bounded` sees activeAdmissions 1 instead of 0 at line 211. 330 pass, 2 fail. Running rolling test remained safely on prior e5327cf immutable result. Fix deterministic runtime/test settlement and injected clock semantics under loaded suite; do not raise sleeps, weaken bounds/assertions, or skip tests. Run repeated focused plus final uncached full npm/Nix after all changes.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Enforce version, tag, changelog, package, and Nix release invariants
description: Automate agreement between package.json, package-lock, src/version.ts, flake.nix and a v-prefixed release tag; require a dated changelog entry; verify the clean packed and Nix artifacts report that version; and make the release workflow fail before publication on mismatch. Include rollback guidance and keep npm registry publication explicit. No v0.1.0 tag should be cut until the newly audited full-host release blockers are resolved or the artifact is explicitly labeled a scaffold preview.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Protocol v2: host scoped neutral tool adapters without arbitrary extensions
description:
Cacophony child bd-2d5c3c cannot enable project-bound pico-daemon tools against current pi-daemon protocol v1: ResourcePolicy.tools is the literal type "none", validateResources rejects every non-none value as unsupported_resource_policy, and SessionAdapter has no capability transport. Add a general-purpose host-side scoped neutral tool-adapter contract without importing Cacophony concepts. Requirements: versioned descriptor passed at open (owner-local Unix endpoint plus opaque session-bound capability handle, never bearer/PKI/env); fixed allowlisted neutral operations rather than arbitrary extension loading or shell; per-session generation binding; root-confined filesystem semantics; request/idempotency/size/concurrency/time bounds; adapter identity and response validation; no inherited ambient environment; close/restart revocation; cross-session and confused-deputy adversarial tests. Keep protocol v1 no-tools behavior unchanged and default-off. Cacophony will supply the project-bound adapter endpoint/token in bd-2d5c3c after this host contract lands.

---

## Operator rollout context — 2026-07-18

Pi-daemon is now healthy on ms-mac and Aurora is expected to receive a host again. This bead is the canonical standalone-host prerequisite for safe tool-bearing Cacophony logical sessions. Keep the contract product-neutral and generation/session-bound. The first Cacophony canary may be no-tools, but wider worker rollout must not proceed until this adapter seam and Cacophony child `cacophony/bd-2d5c3c` are complete. Harry is bringing up `caco-dev-aur-0` to work this seam.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:ms-mac-cacophony-caco-dev-msm-2

title: Dash session ownership: direct co-opt, safe fork/import, conflict detection and export
description: Implement SessionOwnershipService and session storage policy. Permit only the canonical Pi sessions subdirectory as a narrow data root while keeping auth/config siblings forbidden. New Dash sessions default configurably to normal Pi session storage. Existing sessions support reuse, explicit direct/co-opt with user warning, or fork/import with new UUID. Add owner/symlink/root/cwd checks, source fingerprints, durable idempotent ownership tickets/mappings, cooperative leases, daemon/controller checks, best-effort open-writer observation, pre-write prefix/mtime/inode validation, external_write_conflict fail-closed behavior, and fork recovery. Add export-as-new to stock Pi, guarded unchanged-prefix append-back, export-and-release, atomic owner-private publication, and immediate inventory updates. Never silently overwrite divergent source or create two daemon writers.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Fix Linux package and Pages CI portability
description: Release-blocking CI regressions on authoritative main 562b629: Linux/Nix clean-pack test stages consumer/node_modules/.bin/pi-daemon with a relative link that resolves incorrectly and spawn fails ENOENT; Pages uses actions/jekyll-build-pages, which requires Docker unavailable on self-hosted Nix runners. Make installed-bin fallback linkage portable without weakening package validation, replace Docker-based Pages build with a reproducible Nix/Jekyll or equivalent no-Docker path, and run npm plus Linux/Nix-relevant gates.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1

title: Implement bounded host tool-adapter runtime registry and per-session Pi injection
description: Complementary implementation child of bd-fc8275; contract/schema sibling is bd-5c06cd. Build the product-neutral runtime seam that consumes an already validated HostToolAdapterDescriptor and exposes only the fixed filesystem-neutral operations (`fs.list`, `fs.stat`, `fs.read`, `fs.search`, `fs.write`, `fs.edit`) as Pi custom tools. Own a focused adapter client/registry module plus PiSessionFactory injection and unit tests. Enforce owner-local private Unix socket checks, cwd-root-relative path normalization with symlink/traversal fail-closed behavior, session/generation/host-instance identity on every request and response, bounded NDJSON request/response size, concurrency/queue/time limits, idempotency metadata, adapter identity/version validation, abort propagation, and disposal/revoke on close or session replacement. Never load an extension, expose shell/process/network/remove, inherit ambient environment, or spawn a child process. Protocol v1 and every open with `tools:"none"` must remain exactly no-tools/default-off. Treat the capability handle as secret: no logs, errors, status, details, event payloads, or persistence. Coordinate the exact exported descriptor and operation request shapes with bd-5c06cd owner before editing shared runtime-facing types. Keep protocol/schema/docs, multiplexer/server persistence wiring, root end-to-end conformance, web/dashboard, and scheduler files out of this slice; root owner aurora-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-aur-2 will integrate those.
creator: aurora:pi-daemon:aurora-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-aur-2

bead.title: Dash imported sessions fail restart recovery despite existing managed JSONL
bead.description: Rolling test instance on v0.2.2/current main remains degraded after restart with two retained imported Dash sessions. Recovery reports open_failed twice: dash-rZdce... -> ENOENT and dash-Y8_e... -> session_path_outside_state. Both ownership-v1 records are active/imported and both managedPath JSONL files still exist; provider registry is fully ready. This indicates retained catalog/runtime reconstruction is replaying the original fork/open target or losing resolved managed conversation identity instead of reopening the exact existing managed JSONL. Fix recovery so a succeeded fork/direct ownership mapping reopens its recorded managed conversation path/generation without source replay, prompt/model work, or blind mutation. Preserve durable indeterminate semantics and distinguish genuinely missing managed files from source resolution failures. Add restart acceptance for daemon-owned imported sessions and legacy retained records, including exact model/resource policy, no prompt, and ready host state. Do not delete or rewrite the two operator test records while diagnosing.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1
